title: Senegalia suma
taxonomy: Senegalia suma is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to South America. The plant is classified as a legume, which belongs to the pea family. Senegalia suma is also known as Acacia suma and is part of the Acacia genus, but it is now reclassified under the Senegalia genus. The classification of Senegalia suma reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.
uses: Senegalia suma has been reported to have various medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are used in traditional medicine in certain parts of the world. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of Senegalia suma are also used to treat wounds and infections. Additionally, the plant has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory properties.

intro: Senegalia suma, also known as Garuga puncifolia or Artesia puncifolia, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. Senegalia suma contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
